deduplication.strategy: Conservative - only remove rationally identical rules
deduplication.rationale: Analyzed all rules for rational duplicates. Removed only those expressing identical business logic while preserving rules with meaningful differences in thresholds, contexts, or conditions.
deduplication.analysis_notes: Conservative deduplication applied: only clear, rationally identical restatements were merged. Notably, several near-overlaps were intentionally NOT merged because they add or omit material exceptions/conditions (e.g., the general 6-month flow-aging rule BR_DETERMINES_LOAN_ELIGIBILITY_CONSTRAINT_002_001 lacks the explicit HomeStyle Renovation/SFC 279 exception present in the more detailed flow-aging rules; Phase I environmental screening rules overlap but differ on required question logic and stop/continue criteria). Where one rule was a strict superset of another (e.g., servicing rights payoff/EPD obligations), the superset rule was selected as primary and the partial extracts were merged into it to reduce redundancy while preserving full logic.

dependency_analysis.rationale: Identified prerequisite, sequential, conditional, complementary, contradictory, and override relationships between rules to enable proper execution ordering and conflict resolution.

business_rules.rule_name: Trading margin call threshold $3,000,000 and minimum $50,000
business_rules.description: For MBS trading account margin calls based on price differential, both Fannie Mae’s and the lender’s designated threshold amount are $3,000,000 and both parties’ minimum transfer amount is $50,000, unless otherwise agreed in writing or modified due to a triggering event.
business_rules.conditions: When calculating price differential margin calls for MBS trading account
business_rules.consequences: If below threshold/min transfer, no margin call; if met, margin call allowed
business_rules.exceptions: May differ by written agreement or triggering event modification

source_reference.section_id: C3-7-01 (10/06/2021), Designated Threshold Amount and Minimum Transfer Amount
source_reference.source_text: Fannie Mae’s designated threshold amount and a lender’s designated threshold amount shall each be $3,000,000, unless otherwise agreed to by the parties in writing and/or subject to the occurrence of a triggering event as discussed below. ... Fannie Mae’s minimum transfer amount and a lender’s minimum transfer amount shall each be $50,000, unless otherwise agreed to by the parties in writing and/or subject to the occurrence of a triggering event as discussed below.

examples: Price differential = $3,100,000 in lender’s favor; lender may call for $100,000 ($3.1M-$3.0M).
Price differential = $3,040,000; amount above threshold is $40,000 (<$50,000), so margin call not allowed.
Parties execute written amendment setting threshold to $1,000,000; margin call eligibility follows amended terms.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Trading margin call / collateral demand

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_001_001 sets threshold/minimum transfer for margin calls based on price differential. BR_FANNIE_MAE_PROHIBITION_001_005 states Fannie will not wire cash even with positive price differential if triggering events occur and may modify/eliminate thresholds without notice. That behavior supersedes normal threshold-based expectations for cash movement in those triggering-event conditions.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If triggering events occur, the lender cannot rely on threshold-based mechanics to receive cash wires; liquidity planning and settlement operations must shift to a restricted-cash posture.

business_rules.rule_name: Price differential due same day if notice by 10:00 a.m.
business_rules.description: When Fannie Mae provides notice to satisfy the price differential on the lender’s trades with the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk, the deadline depends on when notice is sent: if notice is sent by 10:00 a.m. on a business day, the lender must satisfy the price differential by close of business that same business day; if notice is sent after 10:00 a.m. on a business day, the lender must satisfy by 12:00 noon on the next business day.
business_rules.conditions: When Fannie Mae sends notice to satisfy a trading account price differential
business_rules.consequences: Failure to meet deadline constitutes nonperformance and may trigger default remedies
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: C3-7-01 (10/06/2021), Default of Lender Under the Terms of Trading Account
source_reference.source_text: Instances of nonperformance that constitute a default include the failure to: ... if notice is sent by Fannie Mae by 10:00 a.m. on a business day, satisfy the price diﬀerential on lender’s trades with the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk by the close of business on that same business day; or if notice is sent by Fannie Mae after 10:00 a.m. on a business day, satisfy the price diﬀerential on lender’s trades with the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk by 12:00 noon on the next business day.

examples: Notice sent 9:45 a.m. Tuesday; lender must wire/settle by COB Tuesday.
Notice sent 10:30 a.m. Wednesday; lender must satisfy by 12:00 noon Thursday.
Notice sent 9:30 a.m. Friday before a Monday holiday; lender must satisfy by COB Friday (business day).
business_rules.enforcement_action: Default declaration; accelerate settlement; UCC secured party remedies

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_001_002 governs the lender’s deadline to satisfy a price differential after Fannie Mae notice. In practice, Fannie Mae would only issue (or the lender would only treat) a margin call notice as actionable when the price differential meets the threshold and minimum transfer rules in BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_001_001 (unless modified/otherwise agreed).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the threshold/minimum transfer logic in BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_001_001 is not satisfied (or is misapplied), the lender may either (a) fail to respond to a valid margin call (default risk) or (b) unnecessarily move funds for a non-actionable call (liquidity/operational error).

business_rules.rule_name: Pass-through rate ≥8% limits pools to 3, 4, or 5 per $1M
business_rules.description: For MBS trades with pass-through rate ≥ 8%, the maximum number of pools permitted depends on original trade amount: ≤ $500,000 allows 3 pools; > $500,000 and < $1,000,000 allows 4 pools; and ≥ $1,000,000 allows 5 pools per each million dollars of trade amount.
business_rules.conditions: When structuring MBS pools for trades with pass-through rate ≥ 8%
business_rules.consequences: If pool count exceeds limit, delivery may be rejected or require restructuring
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: C3-7-04 (05/05/2021), MBS Pass-Through Rate / Original Trade Amount
source_reference.source_text:
MBS Pass-Through Rate Original Trade Amount Maximum Number of Pools 
≥ 8%≤ $500,000 3
> $500,000 and < $1,000,000 4
≥ $1,000,0005 pools per each million dollars of
the trade amount

examples: Trade amount $450,000 at 8.125%: maximum 3 pools.
Trade amount $750,000 at 8.000%: maximum 4 pools.
Trade amount $2,400,000 at 8.50%: maximum 10 pools (5 per $1M; apply to each million).
business_rules.enforcement_action: Delivery rejection / required correction

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: Fannie Majors must be certified by 6:00 p.m. ET 4th-to-last day
business_rules.description: All Fannie Major deliveries must be error-free and certified by 6:00 p.m. Eastern time on the fourth to last business day before the end of the month.
business_rules.conditions: When delivering into Fannie Majors MBS pools for a given month-end cycle
business_rules.consequences: If not certified by deadline, delivery may miss issue month/settlement eligibility
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: A lender may submit data for mortgages in the same Fannie Majors at diﬀerent times and on diﬀerent days of the month, as long as all of the mortgages submitted are the same product type and have the same remittance cycle and servicer. All Fannie Major deliveries must be error-free and certiﬁed by 6:00 p.m. Eastern time on the fourth to last business day before the end of the month.

examples: Fourth-to-last business day is Fri 26th; certification must be completed by 6:00 p.m. ET on the 26th.
Submission on the 26th at 6:05 p.m. ET is late; lender may need to roll delivery to next issue month.
If errors remain at 5:55 p.m. ET, lender must correct and certify before 6:00 p.m. ET to meet requirement.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Delivery delay / missed securitization window

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_001_004 requires Major deliveries be “error-free and certified” by a specific monthly deadline. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_001_003 imposes pool-count limits for high pass-through rate trades; exceeding those limits creates a delivery defect that prevents an “error-free” certification state or forces restructuring before certification.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the pool-count constraint is violated, certification may fail before the monthly cutoff, causing missed issue month/settlement eligibility and requiring pool restructuring and re-certification.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_001_004 imposes a monthly certification deadline for Major deliveries. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_031_006 requires UCD Successful status before delivery of any conventional loan. Therefore, UCD completion must precede (or be satisfied before) the Major delivery certification process can be completed.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If UCD submission fails or is incomplete, Major delivery certification cannot be completed for affected loans/pools, risking missed issue month and settlement eligibility.

business_rules.rule_name: Weighted-average guaranty fee factor rounded 0.01 bp
business_rules.description: To determine the annualized weighted-average guaranty fee factor for an MBS pool (in basis points), the lender multiplies each loan’s issue-date principal balance (or scheduled balance in later months) by the applicable guaranty fee rate (in basis points, adjusted for remittance cycle adjustments and any buyup/buydown), sums the products, divides by the aggregate pool balance (issue-date principal balance or scheduled security balance), and rounds the result to the nearest hundredth of a basis point.
business_rules.conditions: When calculating annualized weighted-average guaranty fee factor for an MBS pool
business_rules.consequences: Correct factor supports accurate remittance; miscalc may cause remittance shortfall/adjustment
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: C3-3-01, Determining the Annualized Weighted-Average Guaranty Fee Factor for a Pool
source_reference.source_text: The annualized weighted-average guaranty fee factor for a pool (expressed in basis points) is determined by multiplying the issue date principal balance of each mortgage loan in the pool (or, in subsequent months, the “scheduled” balance of each mortgage loan) by the applicable guaranty fee rate for the mortgage loan expressed in basis points (adjusted for any remittance cycle adjustments, buyup, or buydown, if applicable), then: summing the products of all the mortgage loans in the pool, dividing that sum by the aggregate issue date principal balance for the pool (or, in subsequent months, by the “scheduled” security balance for the pool), and rounding the result to the nearest hundredth of a basis point.

examples: Pool has two loans: $200,000 at 35 bps and $300,000 at 40 bps; factor = ((200,000*35)+(300,000*40))/500,000 = 38 bps (then round to 0.01 bp).
If a loan’s guaranty fee is adjusted due to a buydown, use the adjusted fee rate (in bps) in the multiplication step.
In a subsequent month, use each loan’s scheduled balance and the pool’s scheduled security balance instead of issue-date balances.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Fee draft/adjustment

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: Monthly guaranty fee remittance divide by 120,000
business_rules.description: To determine the monthly guaranty fee remittance amount for an MBS pool, the lender multiplies the annualized weighted-average guaranty fee factor by the pool balance (aggregate issue-date principal balance or scheduled security balance in later months), divides by 120,000, and rounds the result to two decimal places.
business_rules.conditions: When computing the monthly guaranty fee remittance amount for an MBS pool
business_rules.consequences: Correct calculation ensures proper monthly remittance; errors can create drafting discrepancies
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: C3-3-01, Determining the Monthly Guaranty Fee Remittance for the Pool
source_reference.source_text: The annualized weighted-average guaranty fee factor is then multiplied by the aggregate issue date principal balance of the pool (or, in subsequent months, the “scheduled” security balance for the pool), divided by 120,000, and rounded to two decimal places. The result is monthly guaranty fee remittance for the pool.

examples: Annualized factor 38.25 bps and pool UPB $50,000,000: remittance = 38.25*50,000,000/120,000 = $15,937.50 (rounded to 2 decimals).
If using scheduled security balance $49,800,000 in month 2, use that balance in the same formula.
If the calculation yields $15,937.505, round to $15,937.51.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Fee draft/adjustment

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CALCULATION_003_008 instructs the lender to multiply the “annualized weighted-average guaranty fee factor” by the pool balance and divide by 120,000. That factor is produced by BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CALCULATION_003_007; without it, BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CALCULATION_003_008 cannot be computed.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the factor is not calculated (or is wrong), the remittance amount will be wrong, leading to drafting discrepancies, remittance shortfalls, reconciliations, and potential compensatory adjustments.

business_rules.rule_name: Guaranty fee drafted on 7th day or prior business day
business_rules.description: Fannie Mae drafts monthly guaranty fee remittances on the 7th day of each month; if the 7th is a holiday or weekend, the draft occurs on the preceding business day. The lender must manage its designated custodial bank draft account so the draft can occur as scheduled.
business_rules.conditions: When Fannie Mae drafts guaranty fee remittances from lender’s designated draft account
business_rules.consequences: Sufficient funds enables successful drafting; insufficient funds can trigger collection/escalation
business_rules.exceptions: If 7th is holiday/weekend, draft on preceding business day

source_reference.section_id: C3-3-01, Remitting Guaranty Fees to Fannie Mae
source_reference.source_text: Remitting Guaranty Fees to Fannie Mae On the 7th day of each month (or, if the 7th is a holiday or weekend, on the preceding business day), Fannie Mae will draft guaranty fee remittances from the custodial bank account the lender designates as its draft account.

examples: If the 7th falls on a Sunday, Fannie Mae drafts on Friday the 5th; lender must fund the draft account by that Friday.
If the lender changes its designated draft account, it must ensure the new account is able to be drafted on the next 7th-day cycle.
If the pool has no collection activity, the guaranty fee still drafts on the scheduled date.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Fee draft/collection action

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_003_009 states Fannie Mae drafts monthly guaranty fee remittances on the 7th (or preceding business day) and the lender must manage the custodial bank draft account so the draft can occur. To manage funding and reconciliation, the lender must compute the expected remittance amount via BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CALCULATION_003_008.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the remittance amount is not computed correctly (or not computed in time), the draft account may be underfunded or overfunded, causing failed drafts, collections/escalations, or accounting breaks.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_003_009 expects ongoing drafting of guaranty fee remittances. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_005_010 states that upon a valid OFAC match, the servicer must block/segregate funds and ensure all servicing activities cease, including remittances and disbursements—this directly overrides normal remittance processing.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If OFAC cessation requirements are not applied, the servicer may transmit prohibited payments/remittances and mishandle blocked funds, creating sanctions exposure and severe enforcement risk.

business_rules.rule_name: CPM Eligibility Impact Notice Due Within 5 Business Days
business_rules.description: If the lender becomes aware of information that could impact the project eligibility status reflected in CPM (e.g., significant deferred maintenance or major litigation), it must notify the CPM Management team as soon as practicable but no later than five business days after becoming aware. Before selling a loan secured by a unit in such a project, the lender must confirm the project retains its approved status as of the note date unless the loan received and retains the CPM Approved by Fannie Mae message in DU.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when lender learns new project info affecting CPM eligibility status for an approved project.
business_rules.consequences: If met: CPM can be re-evaluated; sale may proceed if status confirmed. If violated: delivery risk and remedies.
business_rules.exceptions: Confirm-as-of-note-date step not required if DU retains CPM Approved by Fannie Mae message.

source_reference.section_id: B4-2.2-02, Additional Obligations of the Lender for Projects Approved by Fannie Mae
source_reference.source_text: If a lender becomes aware of any information that could impact the eligibility status reflected in CPM (such as, significant deferred maintenance or major litigation), the lender must notify the CPM Management team (see E-1-02, List of Contacts) with the relevant data and information. Fannie Mae will evaluate the new information and its impact on eligibility. Before selling a loan secured by a unit in such a project, the lender must confirm that the project retains its approved status as of the note date, unless a loan has received and retains a CPM Approved by Fannie Mae message in DU. Notification must occur as soon as practicable but no later than five business days after becoming aware of such information.

examples: On Monday, lender learns of newly filed major HOA litigation; lender must notify CPM Management by the following Monday (5 business days).
Lender discovers significant deferred maintenance on an approved project; before selling a loan with note date next week, lender confirms project still approved as of note date.

Loan retains DU message 'project has an Approved by Fannie Mae status in CPM'; lender is not required to re-confirm status as of note date, but still must follow delivery restrictions and other obligations.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan delivery ineligibility / potential repurchase or remedies if defect later identified

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_023_010 requires certain projects to be submitted to PERS as a required review method. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_005_008 governs post-approval monitoring/notification for projects with eligibility status reflected in CPM and requires confirmation of approved status as of note date. Operationally, lenders move from initial required review (PERS) to ongoing CPM governance (notification/confirmation) once the project is approved and tracked.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If PERS-required review is skipped, the project may never be properly approved/tracked, making later CPM confirmation/notification moot and resulting in unit ineligibility for delivery.

dependencies.rationale: BR_005_008 is triggered by information impacting 'project eligibility status reflected in CPM' and requires pre-sale confirmation of approved status. In SFHA attached condo contexts, inadequate HOA RCBAP coverage directly impacts project/loan eligibility; therefore BR_004_004 becomes a prerequisite check/input into the 'confirm retains approved status' step.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_004_004 fails: (1) project may not retain approved CPM status, (2) lender should not proceed with sale/delivery until cured or re-evaluated, (3) delivery defect/remedy exposure if delivered without compliant flood coverage.

dependencies.rationale: BR_005_008 requires confirming a project retains approved status as of note date before sale. For projects subject to Full Review, the approved status is built and maintained on meeting criteria such as owner-occupancy/second home conveyance thresholds and phase limitations (BR_020_008). Thus BR_020_008 is a key input to ongoing status confirmation workflows.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_020_008 fails: (1) project may fail Full Review acceptability, (2) CPM approved status may be lost/incorrect, (3) lender must not proceed with sale until addressed, or faces delivery/repurchase risk.

dependencies.rationale: A project that is not permitted under BR_005_007 cannot be treated as eligible/approved for delivery. Therefore, when executing BR_005_008’s pre-sale confirmation step, the lender must ensure the project does not violate BR_005_007 (or that the stated exception applies).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_005_007 fails: (1) project/unit is ineligible for delivery, (2) BR_005_008 confirmation cannot be satisfied legitimately, (3) selling/delivering the loan would create a delivery defect and remedies exposure.

dependencies.rationale: BR_005_008 obligates the lender to confirm project approved status as of note date before selling a loan (absent certain CPM approvals) and to notify CPM when learning information that could impact eligibility. If an inspection within 3 years exists, BR_011_008 mandates review and sets clear eligibility triggers (critical repairs, evacuation orders, regulatory actions). The lender cannot credibly confirm status or decide on notification without this required review when applicable.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_011_008 is skipped, the lender may sell a loan into an ineligible project (delivery defect) or fail to notify CPM timely, increasing repurchase/remedy risk and reputational/audit findings.

dependencies.rationale: BR_005_008 is triggered by information that could impact CPM project eligibility status (explicit example: deferred maintenance). BR_011_007 defines a major deferred-maintenance threshold that renders a project ineligible. While BR_005_008 can be triggered by other factors (e.g., litigation), critical repairs is a central category of eligibility-impacting information and should be linked for navigation and workflow prompts.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_011_007 is not considered, the lender may fail to recognize deferred maintenance as eligibility-changing information, resulting in late/no CPM notification and increased delivery risk.

business_rules.rule_name: Self-report Potential Breaches Within 60 Days and 500 Loans or 1% Threshold
business_rules.description: For repeated potential breaches related to compliance with laws, notification is required within 60 days of the later of (i) the end of the calendar quarter in which the loans were delivered, or (ii) discovery of the potential breach, and reporting must be done using Loan Quality Connect self-report functionality. If the calculation results in fewer than five potentially affected loans, notification is not required. For Reporting Category 2, reporting requirements apply when the breach could warrant repurchase, or when the number of loans affected by the same type of potential breach delivered in the same calendar quarter does not exceed the lesser of 500 loans or 1% of prior-year deliveries; if such breach could warrant repurchase and is not remedied (or will not be remedied) within 60 days, the seller/servicer must notify Fannie Mae within 60 days; if remedied (or will be remedied) within 60 days, notification is not required.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when seller/servicer identifies potential legal compliance breach affecting delivered loans.
business_rules.consequences: If thresholds met: must self-report timely. If violated: increased repurchase/remedy and governance risk.
business_rules.exceptions: No notice required if fewer than 5 affected loans; or if repurchase-warranting breach is remedied within 60 days.

source_reference.section_id: A3-2-01, Reporting Category 2 (partial) and timing rule
source_reference.source_text: The notification is required within 60 days of the later of: the end of the calendar quarter in which such loans were delivered, or discovery of the potential breach. The seller/servicer must report these loans using the self-report functionality in Loan Quality Connect. Note: If the calculation described above results in fewer than five potentially affected loans, then the seller/servicer does not need to notify Fannie Mae. Reporting Category 2 The following reporting requirements apply when: the potential breach could warrant a repurchase demand, or the number of loans affected by the same type of potential breach delivered in the same calendar quarter does not exceed the lesser of 500 loans or 1% of the total number of loans delivered to Fannie Mae in the prior year.

examples: Lender discovers an ECOA valuation notice issue on Aug 15 affecting 200 loans delivered in Q1; because <500 and <1% (if 70,000 prior-year deliveries), reporting may not be required under Category 2 thresholds, but lender must still evaluate whether repurchase could be warranted.
A repurchase-warranting legal breach is identified on May 1 and cannot be remedied by June 30; lender must notify via Loan Quality Connect within 60 days of May 1 (by June 30).
A potential breach impacts 3 loans; because fewer than 5 potentially affected loans, lender does not need to notify Fannie Mae per the note.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan repurchase demand / indemnification risk (if breach is material)

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_005_009 requires reporting using “Loan Quality Connect self-report functionality.” BR_SELLER_SERVICER_DOCUMENTATION_011_009 requires Seller/Servicers to register for Loan Quality Connect and maintain complete loan files; without registration and file completeness, timely self-reporting and supporting evidence submission is severely compromised.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If not registered or unable to produce complete supporting files, self-reporting can be missed or incomplete, increasing remedies/repurchase exposure and governance findings.

business_rules.rule_name: OFAC Match Notice Due Within 24 Hours and No Delivery to Fannie Mae
business_rules.description: Seller/servicer must establish and maintain an effective OFAC compliance program, may not deliver a loan to Fannie Mae if any borrower is on an OFAC sanctions list, and must periodically screen serviced loans against OFAC sanctions lists. If a valid match is identified, the servicer must notify Fannie Mae Ethics via email within 24 hours of identifying the match, block and segregate funds (including escrow), and ensure all servicing activities cease (including remittances and disbursements).
business_rules.conditions: Applies when delivering loans to Fannie Mae or servicing Fannie Mae loans with potential OFAC list matches.
business_rules.consequences: If met: complies with OFAC obligations. If violated: loan cannot be delivered; escalated enforcement and sanctions risk.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated.

source_reference.source_text: Department of Treasury Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) Regulations• must establish and maintain an effective OFAC compliance program; • must report all instances of penalties (civil or criminal) or enforcement actions for compliance failures or violations related to OFAC requirements to Fannie Mae's Ethics division; • may not deliver a loan to Fannie Mae if any borrower is on one of the sanctions lists maintained by OFAC; and • must periodically screen the loans that it services for Fannie Mae against OFAC’s sanctions lists. If the servicer identifies a valid match, the servicer must notify Fannie Mae Ethics via email within 24 hours of identifying a match

examples: Before delivery, lender screens borrower and finds an OFAC SDN list match; loan must not be delivered to Fannie Mae.
Servicer runs periodic OFAC screening and identifies a valid match at 2:00 PM Tuesday; it must notify Fannie Mae Ethics by 2:00 PM Wednesday (within 24 hours).
After a valid match, servicer blocks/segregates escrow funds and stops remitting P&I and paying taxes/insurance until directed by Fannie Mae.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Suspension/termination risk; refusal to purchase; regulatory enforcement exposure

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_020_010 requires screening employees against exclusion lists (GSA EPL, HUD LDP, FHFA SCP) and removing listed individuals from origination/servicing functions. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_005_010 requires OFAC program controls including borrower screening and cessation actions. Together they form layered sanctions/exclusion compliance controls across people (staff) and parties (borrowers/loans).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If either control is missing, the organization’s sanctions/exclusion compliance posture is incomplete, increasing regulatory risk and contractual noncompliance exposure.

business_rules.rule_name: Seller must deliver records within Fannie Mae deadline
business_rules.description: When Fannie Mae issues a written request to examine mortgage records, the seller/servicer must deliver all requested records to Fannie Mae (or its designee) within the timeframe specified by Fannie Mae. If records are maintained in a non-paper format, the seller/servicer must reproduce them at its own expense. If unable to respond timely, the seller/servicer must provide a reasonable explanation and, if appropriate, evidence that the relevant requirement was satisfied. The seller/servicer is responsible for Fannie Mae losses incurred to enforce record access unless Fannie Mae had no legal right of access.
business_rules.conditions: Applies upon Fannie Mae written request to examine mortgage records
business_rules.consequences: Timely delivery satisfies audit; failure may trigger loss liability/enforcement
business_rules.exceptions: Loss liability exception if Fannie Mae had no legal right of access

source_reference.section_id: A2-4.1-02, General Requirements for Audits of Records
source_reference.source_text: When Fannie Mae sends a written request to a seller/servicer to examine mortgage records, the seller/servicer must deliver all records to Fannie Mae or to whomever Fannie Mae designates within the time frame specified by Fannie Mae. Fannie Mae will not execute any trust receipts for documents it requests and will not pay for their delivery. If the seller/servicer is retaining any of the records in a format other than paper, the seller/servicer must reproduce them at its own expense.

examples: Fannie Mae requests a post-purchase file; lender ships imaged documents by the stated due date and satisfies the request.
Lender stores records electronically; when requested, lender prints/exports and provides them at its own expense within the deadline.
Lender cannot meet the deadline due to a system outage; lender provides a written explanation and supporting evidence of compliance for the items under review.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Indemnification for Fannie Mae losses enforcing record access

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_008_007 requires delivering all requested mortgage records within the specified timeframe and reproducing non-paper records. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_DOCUMENTATION_011_009 requires maintaining a complete loan file and providing electronic copies upon request—this is foundational to meeting record examination requests.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If complete loan files are not maintained or cannot be produced in required formats, the seller/servicer may miss the examination deadline, leading to enforcement and potential loss liability.

business_rules.rule_name: QC Review Requires Loan Quality Connect Registration and Complete E-Files
business_rules.description: Seller/Servicers must register for Loan Quality Connect (QC selection notifications are sent via email from Loan Quality Connect) and must maintain a complete loan file including all documents used to support the underwriting decision. Upon request, the lender must provide electronic copies of the complete loan file as described in the request. If unable to submit in supported format, the lender must obtain Loan Quality Center approval before using an alternate submission method.
business_rules.conditions: Fannie Mae QC selects a loan and requests the lender’s complete loan file
business_rules.consequences: If met, QC submission is compliant; if violated, QC response may be non-compliant
business_rules.exceptions: Alternate format allowed only with Loan Quality Center approval

source_reference.section_id: D2-1-02, Notification of a QC Review + Document Submission Requirements
source_reference.source_text: Lenders are notiﬁed which loans Fannie Mae has selected for QC review via an email sent by Fannie Mae’s Loan Quality Connect. Therefore, lenders are required to register for Loan Quality Connect to obtain access to it. Lenders must maintain a complete loan ﬁle, including all documents used to support the underwriting decision. Upon Fannie Mae’s request, lenders must provide electronic copies of the complete loan ﬁle, as described in the request.

examples: Compliant: Lender receives a Loan Quality Connect email, logs in, and uploads the complete underwriting file as requested.
Non-compliant: Lender is not registered for Loan Quality Connect and misses the QC request email, delaying file delivery.
Conditional: Lender cannot produce supported upload format and uses an alternate method only after Loan Quality Center approval.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan repurchase demand

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_011_009 is a general obligation to retain and furnish all documents supporting underwriting decisions. BR_010_005 specifies a particular required document for loans on private/community streets. Therefore, satisfying BR_011_009 for these loans depends on capturing the BR_010_005 document in the file (or the file will be incomplete for QC/exam).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_010_005 fails: (1) underwriting support documentation is missing, (2) QC submission/record exam responses may be deficient, (3) loan eligibility risk increases due to undocumented access/maintenance obligations.

business_rules.rule_name: Public MBS Disclosures Limited to Prospectus and Supplement Only
business_rules.description: When selling Fannie Mae securities to third parties, the lender may publicly disclose only the information contained in the MBS Prospectus and the applicable Prospectus Supplement for that security. Any additional pool or loan information disclosure to the public outside those documents is prohibited.
business_rules.conditions: Lender sells or offers Fannie Mae MBS securities to third parties
business_rules.consequences: If met, public disclosure is permitted; if violated, disclosure is non-compliant
business_rules.exceptions: None stated in excerpt

source_reference.source_text: The information in the MBS Prospectus and the Prospectus Supplement for that security is the only information about a pool (or the mortgage loans included in a pool) that a lender may disclose to the public.

examples: Compliant: Lender posts only the PoolTalk link and references the MBS Prospectus and Prospectus Supplement content.
Non-compliant: Lender publishes a public spreadsheet listing loan-level borrower details beyond what the prospectus materials provide.
Non-compliant: Lender issues a press release describing additional pool loan characteristics not contained in the prospectus/supplement.
business_rules.extraction_notes: No numeric threshold present; explicit prohibition language is clear.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Indemnification

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: Schedule K-1 income allowed only if distributed or liquid
business_rules.description: Income reported on Schedule K-1 may be considered for qualifying only if the lender obtains documentation verifying either (i) the income was actually distributed to the borrower and is consistent with the level of business income used to qualify, or (ii) the business has adequate liquidity to support the withdrawal of earnings. The lender may use discretion in the method used to confirm adequate liquidity.
business_rules.conditions: When qualifying income includes borrower Schedule K-1 income from business
business_rules.consequences: If documentation exists, K-1 income may be used; if not, exclude K-1 income
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: Income reported on Schedule K-1 can only be considered if the lender obtains documentation verifying: the income was actually distributed to the borrower and is consistent with the level of business income being used to qualify, or the business has adequate liquidity to support the withdrawal of earnings. The lender may use discretion in the method used to conﬁrm the business has adequate liquidity.

examples: Borrower receives K-1 income but distributions are not evidenced: lender must not use K-1 income for qualifying.
Borrower’s K-1 shows earnings and business bank statements support sufficient liquidity: lender may use K-1 income even if not fully distributed.
K-1 income is distributed but distributions are materially lower than earnings used to qualify: lender must reconcile or reduce qualifying income to supported level.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan repurchase demand

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_VALIDATION_012_002 requires documentation verifying K-1 distributions or business liquidity. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_VALIDATION_012_005 provides the standard tax documentation set (returns/transcripts with schedules) that frequently contains K-1s and supporting business financial context used to evaluate distributions/liquidity.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If tax returns/transcripts are missing/incomplete, K-1 income may be improperly included or must be excluded, changing qualifying income and underwriting outcomes.

business_rules.rule_name: Self-employed income generally requires 2-year earnings history
business_rules.description: Fannie Mae generally requires the lender to obtain a two-year history of the borrower’s prior earnings to demonstrate the likelihood that self-employment income will continue to be received.
business_rules.conditions: When underwriting self-employment income as qualifying income
business_rules.consequences: If 2-year history obtained, proceed; if not, must meet <2-year exception rules
business_rules.exceptions: Income may be considered with <2-year history if requirements are met

source_reference.source_text: Fannie Mae generally requires lenders to obtain a two-year history of the borrower’s prior earnings as a means of demonstrating the likelihood that the income will continue to be received.

examples: Borrower has filed 2 years of returns for the current business: satisfies the general 2-year history expectation.
Borrower has only 1 year self-employment history: lender must evaluate under the <2-year history provisions before using income.
Borrower has 3+ years of self-employment: still analyze stability, but 2-year history requirement is met.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan repurchase demand

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_012_003 requires demonstrating likelihood of continued self-employment income via a two-year prior earnings history. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_VALIDATION_012_005 provides the core verification mechanism (two years returns or IRS transcripts) that evidences that history.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If returns/transcripts are not obtained (or are incomplete/illegible), the lender may not be able to substantiate the two-year history, forcing exception analysis or exclusion of self-employment income.

business_rules.rule_name: Less than 2 years self-employment allowed with 12 months returns
business_rules.description: A borrower with less than a two-year history of self-employment may be considered if the borrower’s most recent signed personal and business federal income tax returns reflect a full year (12 months) of self-employment income from the current business, and the loan file contains documentation supporting prior income receipt at the same or greater level in a same-products/services field or similar-responsibilities occupation.
business_rules.conditions: When borrower has <2 years self-employment and income is used to qualify
business_rules.consequences: If 12 months returns + prior income support exist, income may be used; else exclude
business_rules.exceptions: None beyond stated conditional allowance

source_reference.source_text: However, the income of a person who has less than a two-year history of self-employment may be considered, as long as the borrower’s most recent signed personal and business federal income tax returns reﬂect a full year (12 months) of self-employment income from the current business. The loan ﬁle must also contain documentation to support the history of receipt of prior income at the same (or greater) level

examples: Borrower self-employed 18 months; most recent signed returns show 12 months, and prior W-2 income in same trade at equal/higher level: lender may consider income.
Borrower self-employed 14 months; returns cover only 9 months: lender may not use self-employment income under this exception.
Borrower self-employed 20 months; returns cover 12 months but prior employment was in unrelated field with different duties: exception not met without additional support.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_012_003 sets the general requirement: obtain a two-year history for self-employment income. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_EXCEPTION_012_004 defines the pathway when the borrower has less than a two-year history—therefore the exception is triggered by failing/being unable to satisfy the general rule.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the 2-year history requirement is not assessed correctly, the lender may incorrectly apply the exception (using ineligible income) or incorrectly deny/underwrite (excluding eligible income).

business_rules.rule_name: Self-employed income verified with 2 years returns or transcripts
business_rules.description: The lender may verify a self-employed borrower’s employment and income by obtaining signed federal income tax returns filed with the IRS for the past two years (individual and, when applicable, business returns) with all schedules attached. Alternatively, the lender may use IRS-issued transcripts for the most recent two years if the transcripts are complete and legible and include information from all applicable schedules.
business_rules.conditions: When verifying self-employed income used for qualifying in underwriting
business_rules.consequences: If acceptable returns/transcripts obtained, proceed; if not, income not properly verified
business_rules.exceptions: One-year returns may be permitted if additional requirements are met

source_reference.source_text: The lender may verify a self-employed borrower’s employment and income by obtaining from the borrower copies of their signed federal income tax returns (both individual returns and in some cases, business returns) that were ﬁled with the IRS for the past two years (with all applicable schedules attached). Alternatively, the lender may use IRS-issued transcripts of the borrower’s individual and business federal income tax returns that were ﬁled with the IRS for the most recent two years—as long as the information provided is complete and legible and the transcripts include the information from all of the applicable schedules.

examples: Lender obtains signed 2024 and 2025 individual returns with Schedule C and all schedules: satisfies 2-year return option.
Lender uses IRS transcripts for 2024 and 2025 but the transcript omits a required schedule: does not meet transcript completeness requirement.
Lender collects only one year of returns without meeting the five-year business/ownership exception: verification requirement not met.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan repurchase demand

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_VALIDATION_012_005 describes two-year returns/transcripts as the baseline approach. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_EXCEPTION_012_006 permits a reduced documentation set (one year) only under defined longevity/ownership conditions; this is an exception/override to the baseline documentation requirement.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the exception is misapplied, the file may be under-documented (QC findings, underwriting defects) or over-documented (unnecessary borrower friction).

business_rules.rule_name: One year tax returns allowed only with 5-year business and 25% ownership
business_rules.description: The lender may provide one year of personal and business tax returns for self-employed income if: (1) the business has been in existence for five years as reflected on the Form 1003, and (2) the borrower has had an ownership share of 25% or more for the past five years consecutively. For partnerships/S-corps/corporations, the business return must support the Form 1003; if the business existed before the borrower reached 25% ownership, the lender must demonstrate 25%+ ownership for at least five years consecutively. All businesses must be assessed separately for the five-year benchmark when multiple self-employment income sources exist.
business_rules.conditions: When lender seeks to document self-employed income with 1 year of tax returns
business_rules.consequences: If all conditions met, 1-year returns permitted; if not, provide 2 years returns/transcripts
business_rules.exceptions: None beyond stated eligibility to use 1-year returns

source_reference.section_id: B3-3.5-01, Verification of Income (one-year returns option)
source_reference.source_text: The lender may provide one year of personal and business tax returns if the following requirements are met: the business from which the borrower is using self-employed income must have been in existence for ﬁve years as reﬂected on the Form 1003, and the borrower has had an ownership share of 25% or more for the past ﬁve years consecutively, and for partnerships, S corporations and corporations, the federal income tax return for the business must support the information reﬂected on the Form 1003.

examples: Borrower has owned 30% of an S-corp for 6 consecutive years and business exists 6 years: lender may use 1 year of returns (if other requirements met).
Borrower owns 25% now but only 3 years at that level (business exists 10 years): lender may not use 1 year returns; must obtain 2 years.
Borrower has two self-employment sources: one business meets 5-year benchmark, the other does not; lender may apply different return-year requirements per business.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: Verbal VOE within 10 business days or 120 days before note date
business_rules.description: A verbal verification of employment (verbal VOE) must be obtained for each borrower using employment or self-employment income to qualify. The verbal VOE must be obtained within 10 business days prior to the note date for employment income, and within 120 calendar days prior to the note date for self-employment income. Alternatively, the lender may obtain the verbal VOE after closing up to the time of loan delivery; if the verbal VOE (or allowable alternative) cannot be obtained prior to delivery, the loan is ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae.
business_rules.conditions: When borrower uses employment or self-employment income to qualify for the loan
business_rules.consequences: If obtained timely, proceed; if not obtained by delivery, loan is ineligible
business_rules.exceptions: Allowed to obtain after closing up to loan delivery

source_reference.source_text: A verbal veriﬁcation of employment (verbal VOE) must be obtained for each borrower using employment or self-employment income to qualify. The verbal VOE must be obtained within 10 business days prior to the note date for employment income, and within 120 calendar days prior to the note date for self-employment income. Alternatively, the lender may obtain the verbal VOE after closing, up to the time of loan delivery. If the verbal VOE (or allowable alternative) cannot be obtained prior to delivery, the loan is ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae.

examples: W-2 borrower closing on June 20: verbal VOE must be dated within 10 business days before June 20 (or use an allowed alternative within required window).
Self-employed borrower note date June 20: verbal VOE must be dated within 120 calendar days before June 20, unless obtained after closing but before delivery.
Lender cannot obtain VOE or alternative before delivering the loan: loan is ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_012_009 applies when the borrower is using employment or self-employment income to qualify and imposes timing rules for verbal VOE. In most workflows, the lender first validates self-employment income via acceptable returns/transcripts (BR_SELLER_SERVICER_VALIDATION_012_005) and then completes/refreshes VOE within the required window before note date (or by delivery).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If sequencing breaks (VOE not refreshed within required window), the loan becomes ineligible at delivery even if income documentation was otherwise acceptable.

business_rules.rule_name: Seller must self-report selling warranty breach within 30 days
business_rules.description: If, after conducting due diligence, the lender determines that a breach of a selling warranty has likely occurred, the lender must notify Fannie Mae within 30 days using the self-report functionality in Loan Quality Connect.
business_rules.conditions: After due diligence, lender determines a selling warranty breach likely occurred
business_rules.consequences: Failure to timely report may increase remedy/sanction risk under the contract
business_rules.exceptions: None stated in the cited passage

source_reference.section_id: A2-2-01, Lender Reporting Requirements for Breach of Selling Warranty
source_reference.source_text: The lender must notify Fannie Mae within 30 days if, after conducting due diligence, it determines that a breach of a selling warranty has likely occurred, by using the self-report functionality in Loan Quality Connect .

examples: QC review identifies misstatement of occupancy; lender completes due diligence and self-reports via Loan Quality Connect within 30 days: meets reporting requirement.
Lender discovers missing title exception documentation but does not report for 75 days: violates the 30-day reporting requirement.
Third-party fraud is discovered; lender determines a selling warranty breach likely occurred and self-reports within 30 days: meets requirement even though lender did not commit fraud.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Indemnification or loan repurchase demand

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_013_007 mandates notice using the self-report functionality in Loan Quality Connect. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_DOCUMENTATION_011_009 mandates registration for Loan Quality Connect and maintaining a complete loan file needed to support the report.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If LQC registration/file readiness is missing, the lender may fail to notify within 30 days, increasing contractual remedy exposure.

business_rules.rule_name: Value acceptance may be exercised only if offer ≤ 4 months old
business_rules.description: A lender may only exercise value acceptance if the final DU submission resulted in a value acceptance offer, no appraisal is obtained for the transaction, and the offer is not more than four months old on the date of the note and mortgage. If exercised, the lender must include Special Feature Code (SFC) 801 at delivery.
business_rules.conditions: Seller/Servicer intends to deliver a loan using DU value acceptance
business_rules.consequences: If conditions met, deliver without appraisal; if violated, must order appraisal
business_rules.exceptions: None stated in the exercise criteria excerpt

source_reference.source_text: A lender may only exercise value acceptance if the ﬁnal submission of the loan caseﬁle to DU resulted in a value acceptance oﬀer, an appraisal is not obtained for the transaction, and the value acceptance oﬀer is not more than four months old on the date of the note and the mortgage. Lenders that elect to exercise value acceptance must include SFC 801 at delivery.

examples: DU value acceptance offer dated 3 months before note date and no appraisal ordered → lender may exercise value acceptance and deliver with SFC 801.
DU value acceptance offer is 5 months old at note date → lender may not exercise; must obtain an appraisal.
Lender orders an appraisal after receiving value acceptance → lender may not exercise value acceptance for that transaction.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan repurchase demand / remedies for breach of requirements

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: Value acceptance plus data requires API submit before note and 12-month data
business_rules.description: A lender may only exercise value acceptance + property data when the final DU submission resulted in an eligibility message for the option, the property data collection is submitted to the Property Data API prior to the note date, no appraisal is obtained, and the offer is not more than four months old on the date of the note and mortgage. The lender must include SFC 774 at delivery. Property data collection is valid for 12 months from the collection date and must be performed prior to the note date.
business_rules.conditions: Seller/Servicer uses value acceptance + property data instead of an appraisal
business_rules.consequences: If satisfied, appraisal not required; if not, must obtain hybrid/desktop/traditional
business_rules.exceptions: If offer lost, property data may support a hybrid appraisal per Guide

source_reference.source_text: A lender may only exercise value acceptance + property data when the ﬁnal submission of the loan caseﬁle to DU resulted in an eligibility message for value acceptance + property data, property data collection is submitted to the Property Data API prior to the note date, an appraisal is not obtained for the transaction, and the oﬀer is not more than four months old on the date of the note and mortgage. Lenders that elect to exercise value acceptance + property data must include Special Feature Code 774 at loan delivery. The property data collection is only valid for 12 months from date of collection and must be performed prior to the note date.

examples: Property data collected 2 weeks before note date and submitted to API before note date; offer is 2 months old → lender may deliver without appraisal using SFC 774.
Property data collected after the note date → lender cannot exercise this option; must obtain appraisal solution required by DU.
Property data collected 13 months ago (even if resubmitted) → invalid; lender must obtain new property data collection or appraisal.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan repurchase demand / delivery rejection risk

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_018_007 covers DU value acceptance without appraisal and requires SFC 801 at delivery. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_018_008 covers value acceptance + property data, requires Property Data API submission and SFC 774. A loan should follow the path corresponding to DU’s eligibility message and whether property data collection was submitted; using the wrong path or coding both can create delivery defects.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the lender misclassifies the waiver type, it may deliver with the wrong SFC and missing prerequisite artifacts (e.g., missing Property Data API submission), causing delivery findings, repurchase risk, or forced appraisal.

business_rules.rule_name: eNote Registration Must Occur Within 1 Business Day After Signing
business_rules.description: Lenders must have process controls to ensure eNotes are registered in the MERS eRegistry as soon as possible after the tamper-evident seal is applied, but no later than one business day after signing. The MERS eRegistry record must show the originating lender (the lender whose name is on the eNote) as the first Controller. If trade/assumed names are used on eNotes, the legal entity name must also be included on the eNote, and the initial MERS eRegistry registration must reflect the legal entity name as the initial Controller. Failure to comply could render the loan ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when originating and closing an eMortgage with an eNote.
business_rules.consequences: If registered timely with correct Controller, compliant; if not, may be ineligible for sale.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated.

source_reference.source_text: Lenders must have process controls in place to ensure eNotes are registered in the MERS eRegistry as soon as possible after the tamper-evident seal has been applied, but no later than one business day after signing. The record in the MERS eRegistry must reflect the originating lender - the lender whose name is on the eNote - as the first Controller. The following applies to lenders using tradenames or assumed names on eNotes: the legal entity name must also be included on the eNote... and the initial MERS eRegistry registration must reflect the legal entity name as the initial Controller... Failure to comply with these requirements could render the loan ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae.

examples: Borrower signs Tuesday; lender registers eNote in MERS eRegistry by Wednesday (next business day) with lender as first Controller → compliant.
Borrower signs Friday; lender registers the following Tuesday (2 business days later) → violates timing requirement; loan may be ineligible.
eNote shows 'XYZ Mortgage' only; legal entity 'ABC Financial, Inc.' not included and Controller set to XYZ → violates tradename/legal-entity requirement.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae.

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: Employees on GSA HUD or FHFA Lists Cannot Work Fannie Loans
business_rules.description: The seller/servicer must follow a written procedure for checking all employees (including management) involved in origination or servicing of mortgage loans against the GSA Excluded Parties List (EPL), HUD Limited Denial of Participation (LDP) List, and FHFA Suspended Counterparty Program (SCP) list. If at time of hire or any time later an individual is determined to be on any of these lists, the seller/servicer may not permit that employee to manage or perform origination or servicing functions on loans sold to Fannie Mae, regardless of the reason the individual is on the list.
business_rules.conditions: Applies at hire and ongoing for staff supporting Fannie Mae loan lifecycle.
business_rules.consequences: If listed, employee must be removed from covered functions; failure is noncompliance.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated; applies regardless of reason for listing.

source_reference.section_id: A3-4-03, Fraud Prevention Requirements - Hiring Practices
source_reference.source_text: The seller/servicer must follow a written procedure for checking all employees, including management, involved in the origination or servicing of mortgage loans (including application through closing and servicing) against the U.S. General Services Administration (GSA) Excluded Parties List (EPL), the HUD Limited Denial of Participation List (LDP List), and the Federal Housing Finance Agency’s (FHFA) Suspended Counterparty Program (SCP) list. If, at the time of hire or any time later, the seller/servicer has determined an individual is on the GSA, LDP, or SCP list, the seller/servicer may not permit that employee to manage or perform origination or servicing functions on loans sold to Fannie Mae, irrespective of the reason the individual is on such list.

examples: New processor is screened at hire and not on any list → may work Fannie Mae loans.
Underwriter later appears on FHFA SCP list; lender immediately removes underwriter from Fannie Mae loan underwriting → compliant response.
Lender learns a closer is on HUD LDP list but continues assigning closings on Fannie Mae loans → violation and heightened repurchase/termination risk.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Suspension/termination risk; loan repurchase/indemnification exposure.

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: Seller Must Return Servicing Docs Within 5 Days
business_rules.description: When the Servicer requires Seller execution of servicing-related documentation, Seller must execute and return the documentation as promptly as reasonably feasible, but in no event more than five (5) days after delivery.
business_rules.conditions: Execution by Seller is required for servicing documentation after delivery
business_rules.consequences: If met, servicing transfer proceeds; if violated, transfer delays and disputes
business_rules.exceptions: Alternative: Seller may provide POA allowing Servicer execution

source_reference.source_text: alternative when execution by Seller is required, Seller shall, as promptly as reasonably feasible, but in no event exceeding ﬁve (5) days after delivery, execute and

examples: Servicer sends a satisfaction document to Seller on Monday; Seller must return executed document by Saturday (5 days).
Servicer requests Seller-signed assignment correction delivered on June 1; Seller returns signed version by June 5 (compliant).
Instead of signing each document, Seller provides a power of attorney enabling Servicer to execute required documents (alternative compliance).
business_rules.enforcement_action: Operational remedy; potential contractual dispute

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: Escrowed Taxes Must Be Paid Within 30 Days After Transfer
business_rules.description: If the mortgage loan requires the mortgagor to escrow funds, Seller must pay all real estate taxes that are due and payable on or before the Purchase Date and within thirty (30) days after the Purchase Date. Seller must not pay any bill for which Seller has not specifically retained escrow funds. If the loan does not require escrow, Seller must cause the mortgagors to pay the described real estate taxes.
business_rules.conditions: Servicing rights transferred; taxes due and payable and escrow requirement applies
business_rules.consequences: If met, tax obligations stay current; if violated, tax delinquency and transfer issues
business_rules.exceptions: If no escrow required, Seller must cause mortgagors to pay taxes

source_reference.source_text: Real Estate Tax Payment . In the event the Mortgage Loan requires the mortgagor to escrow funds, Seller shall pay all real estate taxes that are due and payable (i) on or before the Purchase Date, and (ii) within thirty (30) days after the Purchase Date. Seller agrees to follow transfer procedures with respect to real estate tax service contracts in a manner described in the Servicing Transfer Instructions. In addition, Seller shall not pay any bill for which Seller has not specifically retained escrow funds pursuant to Section 6.2 herein. In the event the Mortgage Loan does not require the mortgagor to escrow funds, Seller shall cause the mortgagors to pay the above-described real estate taxes.

examples: Escrowed loan has property taxes due 10 days after Purchase Date; Seller must pay within 30 days after Purchase Date.
Tax bill arrives but Seller did not retain escrow funds under Section 6.2; Seller must not pay that bill (non-payment required).
Non-escrow loan has taxes due after transfer; Seller must ensure mortgagor pays the taxes as described.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Operational remedy; potential contractual dispute

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_021_002 requires paying real estate taxes under certain escrow/transfer conditions. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_005_010 requires blocking/segregating funds (including escrow) and ceasing servicing activities including disbursements upon a valid OFAC match. That creates an exception state overriding routine tax disbursement obligations until properly handled under OFAC controls.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the lender continues tax disbursements from blocked escrow, it can violate OFAC requirements. If it ceases disbursements without appropriate OFAC handling/notification, it can create tax delinquency risk—requiring a controlled, documented resolution path.

business_rules.rule_name: Loan File Due in 3 Business Days or $500 Late Fee
business_rules.description: Within three (3) Business Days after the Purchase Date, Seller must furnish Servicer (or designated document custodian) a complete loan file as described in the Fannie Mae Selling Guide (unless otherwise directed by Fannie Mae), must retain a copy of the complete loan file per the Fannie Mae Servicing Guide, and may be charged a late fee up to $500.00 per mortgage loan if Seller fails to comply.
business_rules.conditions: After Purchase Date when servicing rights are transferred and file delivery required
business_rules.consequences: If met, Servicer receives compliant file; if violated, late fee up to $500/loan
business_rules.exceptions: Complete file delivery may differ if directed otherwise by Fannie Mae

source_reference.source_text: Within three (3) Business Days after the Purchase Date, Seller at its own expense shall furnish Servicer or the designated document custodian, as applicable, with the following: ... (b) File. A complete loan file as described in the Fannie Mae Selling Guide to Servicer unless otherwise directed by Fannie Mae. Seller must also retain a copy of the complete loan file and maintain that copy in accordance with the requirements of the Fannie Mae Servicing Guide and any other applicable requirements set forth therein. ... (d) Late Fee . If Seller fails to comply with this Section, Servicer may, in its sole discretion, charge Seller a late fee of up to $500.00 per mortgage loan.

examples: Purchase Date is Tuesday; Seller must deliver the complete file by Friday (3 business days) to avoid late fee exposure.
Seller delivers file on day 6; Servicer may charge $500 late fee for that mortgage loan.
Fannie Mae directs an alternative file routing; Seller follows that instruction instead of standard delivery destination.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Late fee up to $500 per mortgage loan

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_021_003 requires furnishing a complete loan file within 3 business days after purchase date and retaining a copy. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_DOCUMENTATION_011_009 defines the expectation that the lender maintains a complete loan file including all documents used to support the underwriting decision.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the lender does not maintain a complete file, it cannot timely furnish a complete transfer file, leading to late fees and servicing transfer defects.

business_rules.rule_name: Annual audited financial statement due within 90 days
business_rules.description: The Seller/Servicer must submit its annual Audited Financial Statement (AFS) within 90 days after the end of its fiscal year. Failure to timely provide required financial statements and information may result in Fannie Mae declaring a breach of the Lender Contract.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to every Seller/Servicer fiscal year-end AFS submission to Fannie Mae
business_rules.consequences: If late/not provided, Fannie Mae may declare a Lender Contract breach
business_rules.exceptions: See separate exceptions for supervised depository institutions and HUD audits

source_reference.source_text: The seller/servicer must demonstrate its ﬁnancial adequacy to Fannie Mae. To accomplish this, Fannie Mae requires the seller/servicer to submit ﬁnancial statements and other information described in this topic. A seller/servicer’s failure to timely provide the required information may result in Fannie Mae declaring a breach of the Lender Contract. A seller/servicer must submit their annual Audited Financial Statement (AFS) within 90 days after the end of the seller/servicer’s ﬁscal year.

examples: Seller/Servicer fiscal year ends 12/31; AFS must be submitted by 03/31; submission on 04/15 is a late submission exposing a breach finding.
Seller/Servicer fiscal year ends 06/30; AFS due by 09/28; failure to submit can trigger Lender Contract breach remedies.
Seller/Servicer submits AFS by day 75 after fiscal year end; requirement satisfied and no breach risk on timeliness.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Breach of Lender Contract

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: Form 1002 quarterly due 30 days; Dec 31 due 60
business_rules.description: If the Seller/Servicer is a mortgage banker, subsidiary of a federally supervised depository institution, housing finance agency, or REIT, it must submit Form 1002 at the end of each calendar quarter, certified by the CEO, CFO, or equivalent. The Seller/Servicer must electronically submit within 30 days for the March 31, June 30, and September 30 reports, and within 60 days for the December 31 report. Each report must include only the financial data related to the quarterly reporting period.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Seller/Servicer type requires Form 1002 quarterly reporting
business_rules.consequences: Late/inaccurate quarterly reporting may support eligibility actions or breach
business_rules.exceptions: Non-calendar accounting cycles may be used if report includes only required quarter

source_reference.source_text: In addition to the AFS, the seller/servicer must submit a Mortgage Bankers’ Financial Reporting Form (Form 1002) that has been certiﬁed by the chief executive oﬃcer, the chief ﬁnancial oﬃcer, or equivalent at the end of each calendar quarter. Each report must include only the ﬁnancial data related to the quarterly reporting period for which the report is being submitted. The seller/servicer must electronically submit this information within 30 days for the March 31, June 30, and September 30 reports and within 60 days for the December 31 report.

examples: For Q1 ending 03/31, Form 1002 must be submitted by 04/30; submission on 05/10 violates the deadline.
For Q4 ending 12/31, Form 1002 must be submitted by 03/01 (60 days); submission on 02/15 meets the rule.
If a Seller/Servicer uses a non-standard quarterly accounting cycle, it may keep its methodology but must ensure each report contains data for only the required quarter.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Breach of Lender Contract

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: Form 1002A monthly due 30 days; skip quarter 3rd month
business_rules.description: If the Seller/Servicer is a large non-depository Seller/Servicer, it must electronically submit Form 1002A within 30 days of the end of each month, certified by the CEO, CFO, or equivalent. Each report must include only the financial data related to the month preceding the month of submission. The Seller/Servicer is not required to provide a monthly report for the third month of any fiscal quarter because the data will be captured in the quarterly report.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to large non-depository Seller/Servicers subject to monthly Form 1002A
business_rules.consequences: Missing/late reports may drive enhanced oversight or eligibility actions
business_rules.exceptions: No monthly report required for each third month of any fiscal quarter

source_reference.source_text: In addition to the required quarterly reporting, a large non-depository seller/servicer must electronically submit a Mortgage Bankers' Financial Reporting Short Form (Form 1002A) within 30 days of the end of each month. The report must be certiﬁed by the chief executive oﬀer, the chief ﬁnancial oﬃcer, or equivalent. Each report must include only the ﬁnancial data related to the month preceding the month in which the report is being submitted. The seller/servicer is not required to provide a monthly report for each third month of any ﬁscal quarter, as the data will be captured in the quarterly report.

examples: A large non-depository Seller/Servicer’s January month-end is 01/31; Form 1002A must be submitted by 03/02 (30 days after 01/31).
For a fiscal quarter April–June, the Seller/Servicer submits monthly reports for April and May but does not submit a June monthly report in July because June is the third month of the quarter.
If a monthly report includes two months of data (e.g., April+May), it violates the requirement to include only the prior month’s data.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Breach of Lender Contract

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: Form 582 update within 90 days; notify changes in 5 days
business_rules.description: The Seller/Servicer must update Form 582 electronically no later than 90 days after the end of its fiscal year. If lender record information changes, the Seller/Servicer must update Form 582 and provide written notification by email to the designated mailbox within five business days of the occurrence of specified events (e.g., actions pending/threatened that could reasonably be expected to have a material adverse effect).
business_rules.conditions: Applies to annual eligibility maintenance and upon any lender record information change
business_rules.consequences: Failure/late notice increases eligibility risk and may lead to Fannie Mae action
business_rules.exceptions: None stated in excerpt

source_reference.source_text: The Lender Record Information ( Form 582 ) provides information needed to verify that the seller/servicer continues to meet Fannie Mae's basic eligibility requirements as well as certiﬁcations regarding compliance with our requirements. The seller/servicer must update its Form 582 electronically no later than 90 days after the end of the seller/servicer’s ﬁscal year.
